author: @tomkelly8827
text: I wonder, do you need to have it as a horizontal axis turbine or could it be vertical? If it was vertical axis, then some opposing magnets could be used as a type of bearing to help it spin more freely with less friction

author: @johnwilson3668
text:
Brilliant idea to make a flywheel generator . If the flywheel is allowed to free wheel it will produce energy even when the wind stops for a few seconds until the turbine catches up with it. 
It is like peddling a cycle down hill however fast you pedal ,you cannot keep up with it .Stop peddling and you still carry on at the same speed . Momentum is an incredible force.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: spot on mate my thought too

author: @NorthernKitty
text:
I love the way you approached this video!!!  Since I can relate very well to: "one of my habits is to talk about things as if I know what I'm talking about."  I have a very nasty habit of doing exactly the same thing, which is in part why I chose the name "Cats Pajamas" - to help remind me not to take myself so seriously when I'm commenting.  (Not sure that it works, but it was worth the effort! 😋)  Watching your video today was an even better reminder!!  I think we learn as many lessons on how to have a healthy attitude from your videos as we do technical knowledge.  😊

I think it's inevitable to develop such a habit in competitive environments, such as academic circles, executive management, athletics or politics.  Anywhere you aren't allowed to display the slightest bit of doubt or uncertainty without someone seizing on it to steamroll you.  It's a somewhat benign habit under most circumstances, so long as there is self-awareness of doing it and a readiness to admit it (especially to yourself) when it gets in the way of doing things right.  

One of my mentor's used to tell me, "when you're absolutely certain you know all the answers, that's when you should realize you're completely ignorant on the subject."  😄  That and "the person you should doubt most is the person who is without doubt."  😁

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: children display that attitude - it is the certainty of the ignorant lol. I have another habit that helps me not get steam rollered - I can a bit brutal when someone says something silly - i don't mean to be I am just not good with folks talking rubbish - my bad as they say lol - oh - I also don't really listen to stupid - I just go and do what I think is right lol

replies.author: @NorthernKitty
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I lack the latter two habits, but VERY often wish I had them!  😋  I try too hard to make everyone happy, without realizing that some people are determined to be unhappy.  Too often (especially in the age of social media) it turns out to be a wasted effort.  I'm truly perplexed by that, as life is already difficult enough without making it even more difficult for ourselves with pessimism.  It's why I treasure your videos so much - you approach every project with a rich dose of optimism, and find a way to enjoy any and every result.  That's the most valuable thing you are teaching people here, this particular video being a textbook example.  And yet sometimes in the comments I see people completely missing that message.

author: @hanslepoeter5167
text:
Nice. You must be the busiest man on the planet producing something like that every other day. 
What I don't really get is why these kind of turbines spin in the first place. If you think about it the wind hits the blade in front producing force clockwise, then hits the blade at the back producing force anti clockwise. Net result would be zero. I guess wind direction change inside the turbine due to the blades in front play's an important role. That would mean it's far from efficient. Also if you put a motor on it no matter how hard it spins it will not generate wind in a specific direction due to symmetry, indicating it is not that efficient as a turbine as well imho. How about making an active one  where all blades can rotate in respect to the cable drum and all blades rotate when spinning to the optimal angle in respect to the wind ? Maybe make one with 2 or 3 blades as a proof of concept ? That would be an interesting project. ( But not easy :-) )

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it would be an interesting project - but more interesting to me is that as it spins it should be telling you something - that is what you are saying might have some element of error in it - I would think that it is that you are assuming a symmetry that isn't there. I don't know I am guessing but my guess is based on the observation of it spinning in a wind - of course rolling cylinders will create lift and pressure differentials between sides - so that might have a role to play

author: @Xx-xd3zo
text:
For someone who doesn't know what he's doing...
You sure do make a lot of things work.  Lol!  
Far better than most who "know what they're doing"!
One is far better off with someone like Rob who "doesn't know what he's doing" than most who "do".  :)  I used to work with the latter daily.

I must be some kind of free energy...erem....enthusiast.  Because I see stuff like that drum when I'm driving around, or I'm watching that thing spin, and I'm thinking, "Ok, magnets THERE.  And coils down there..."  It's a sickness.  I know.  But at least its clean/carbon emission free.

The weight of that thing really does smooth out the gusts.  Great lesson.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
lol - I don't video th failures mate - and there are quite a few of them! - I truly lack respect for folks who know everything - to me it is the mindset of children and in children I have no problem with it - but in adults - they should have learnt by that age - there is no knowing everything - soap box - sorry lol
I think looking os the thing mate - awesome!

@ThinkingandTinkering - Lol!  You said it.  But...is there going to be a compilation of the things that didn't go so hot?  Sort of like a Greatest Non-Hits?  Just wondering.

author: @foobargorch
text: if you find more drums it would be cool to compare different blade configurations, iirc there's two main designs, more like a scoop and more like an airfoil and either can be more efficient depending on shape, diameters and wind speed distribution, i would guess what you built is somewhere in between the two approaches

replies.author: @foobargorch
replies.text:
btw there's a cool industrial tool for pond aeration which involves a spinning vertical pipe whose underwater an outlet has small flaps that centrifugally expell water and suck in air through the pipe... this device is used to treat wastewater and iirc also for remediation. i don't have them anymore but when i had a few 200L barrels as part of the garden i was always curious to try building an aerator with be a VAWT made out of a large coffee can (there are various of plans online) designed for relatively high RPM, mounted directly on a hollow shaft that terminates in such an outlet as a simple design (could be on a bouey or some deep edge), the deeper it is the higher the resistance so i think that's the only adjustment that would needed to be made, and it's probably best to err on the side of smaller, because depth is also better for stability and even aeration.

i know large pond aerator wind turbines are a thing and usually quite complicated and costly (like thousands of dollars, designed for things like a dam or pond on a farm), and pumps need to be redundant and may still have a single point of failure without an uninterruptable power supply, so having a wind powered backup with few moving parts and no valves or anything seemed like a nice idea, especially in combination with say a solar powered air or recirculation pump, ensuring the fish stay alive even if power goes out for more than a few hours. anyway i don't have those barrels anymore so it'll be a while before i would be able to try building something but i thought in an case anyone else likes the idea i might share it.

replies.text: go for it mate - sounds to me. like you know what you want to see

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it really sounds to me like you should be experimenting mate

author: @jamest.5001
text: I have thought about using a couple of those as a water wheel, either a undershot wheel, or a I guess gravity wheel, where it is gears overdriven. To the point it spins about 10 rpm. Having troughs that fill with water, about 10 gallons, in each trough on the wheel. About 2.5 at best depending on the diameter, about 45killo per.  At about 24" that should spin a decent amount of tq,  it would have to spin slow enough to fill the through, before going to the next. I have a small spring, that all but stops mid summer, with no rain, it would probably be measured in rph! Make like 60 -80 rph hourly rotation, ha-ha. But spin a jackshaftck about 45 rpm, spinning a alternator about 500-1k rpm.. It would be about a 100:1, with a 96" wheel, with 80lbs on about 6 troughs, it would have alot of force!or be heavy anyway!
